title: PLANNING IN STOCHASTIC ENVIRONMENTS WITH A LEARNED MODEL
authors: Ioannis Antonoglou, Julian Schrittwieser, Sherjil Ozair, Thomas Hubert, David Silver
tldr: Introduces Stochastic MuZero, extending the MuZero algorithm to learn and plan with stochastic models, achieving state-of-the-art performance in stochastic domains like 2048 and Backgammon without sacrificing its superhuman strength in Go.

sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.quote: However, value equivalent methods such as MuZero have in practice been limited to a deterministic class of models, which severely limits their applicability. Many environments are inherently stochastic and may be poorly approximated by a deterministic model.

sections.heading: Related Work
sections.content:
Prior works in model-based RL fall into several categories:
- **Observation models** (e.g., Dyna-style models) explicitly learn environment dynamics by predicting future observations and rewards, but struggle with high-dimensional inputs and accumulating errors.
- **Latent models** (e.g., standard MuZero) predict values, policies, and rewards in a latent space, which is highly effective but assumes deterministic dynamics.
- **Stochastic latent models** (e.g., VQ-VAE for planning) attempt to handle stochasticity, but previously failed to match MuZero's performance in combined planning and learning, or required supervised expert data.

steps.label: Afterstates
steps.description: Transitions are factored into a deterministic step to an 'afterstate' (post-action), followed by a stochastic chance transition.

steps.label: VQ-VAE
steps.description: Models chance outcomes using a Vector Quantised Variational AutoEncoder with a constant codebook of size M.

steps.label: Stochastic MCTS
steps.description: Extends MCTS by interleaving decision nodes and chance nodes, sampling chance codes from the learned prior distribution.

sections.heading: Results & Validation
rows: Outperformed previous SotA (Jaskowski 2016) using less data; matched AlphaZero.
Matched GNUbg Grandmaster and AlphaZero; outperformed standard deterministic MuZero.
Matched the performance of standard MuZero, proving it doesn't sacrifice deterministic strength.

left.title: Algorithmic Innovations
left.content:
- **Factored Dynamics:** Separates transitions into deterministic afterstates and stochastic chance outcomes.
- **VQ-VAE Integration:** Learns discrete chance codes end-to-end within the RL loop.

right.title: Impact
right.content:
- Extends MuZero to stochastic and partially observed domains.
- Removes the need for perfect simulators in complex stochastic games like Backgammon and 2048.

key_takeaways: Standard MuZero struggles in environments where transitions are inherently stochastic or appear stochastic due to partial observability.
By factoring transitions into afterstates and chance outcomes using a VQ-VAE, Stochastic MuZero effectively models uncertainty.
The approach matches perfect-simulator AlphaZero in stochastic games without losing its dominance in deterministic ones like Go.
